name: BloodHound Data Collection
description: Collects Active Directory data (users, groups, sessions, ACLs, trusts) for attack path analysis in BloodHound.
expected_output:
INFO: Connecting to LDAP server: dc01.corp.local
INFO: Found 1532 users
INFO: Found 89 groups
INFO: Done in 00:02:34
use_case: Map Active Directory attack paths — find shortest path to Domain Admin, identify misconfigurations.
common_mistakes: Forgetting to import into BloodHound GUI

name: DCSync / Secrets Dump
description: Dumps all domain password hashes via DCSync (replicating DC behavior) or from SAM/SYSTEM/NTDS.dit.
use_case: Extract all domain hashes after gaining Domain Admin or DCSync privileges. Used for pass-the-hash attacks.
prerequisites: Domain Admin or DCSync privileges (Replicating Directory Changes)
detections: DRS replication from non-DC source (Event ID 4662)
common_mistakes: Not saving output for offline cracking

name: Remote Command Execution (PsExec)
description: Executes commands on remote Windows systems using SMB. Creates a service, uploads executable, runs it, cleans up.
expected_output:
Impacket v0.11.0 - Copyright 2023 Fortra
[*] Requesting shares on 192.168.1.10...
[*] Found writable share ADMIN$
[*] Uploading file...
Microsoft Windows [Version 10.0.19045]
C:\Windows\system32>
use_case: Get an interactive shell on a remote Windows machine using valid credentials or NTLM hash.
common_mistakes: Using on monitored systems without evasion

name: SMB Enumeration with NetExec
description: Enumerate SMB shares, users, and check credentials across multiple hosts simultaneously.
expected_output:
SMB  192.168.1.10  445  DC01  [+] corp.local\user:pass
SMB  192.168.1.10  445  DC01  [+] Shares: ADMIN$, C$, IPC$, NETLOGON, SYSVOL, SharedDocs
use_case: Mass enumeration of SMB across a network — check credential validity, list shares, find writable shares.

name: LLMNR/NBT-NS Poisoning
description: Poisons LLMNR, NBT-NS, and mDNS responses to capture NTLMv2 hashes from machines on the local network.
expected_output:
[+] Listening for events...
[*] [LLMNR] Poisoned answer sent to 192.168.1.50
[+] NTLMv2 Hash: user::CORP:1122334455667788:ABC123...
use_case: Capture NTLMv2 hashes passively by poisoning name resolution requests on the local network.
detections: Multiple LLMNR/NBT-NS responses from single host
remediation: Disable LLMNR and NBT-NS via GPO

name: Kerberos User Enumeration
description: Enumerate valid domain usernames via Kerberos pre-authentication without triggering account lockouts.
expected_output:
2024/01/15 10:30:00 >  [+] VALID USERNAME: administrator@corp.local
2024/01/15 10:30:00 >  [+] VALID USERNAME: svc_sql@corp.local
2024/01/15 10:30:01 >  [+] VALID USERNAME: j.smith@corp.local
use_case: Discover valid usernames without authentication — doesn't trigger lockout policies.
detections: High volume of Kerberos AS-REQ (Event ID 4768)
Failed pre-auth events for non-existent users
common_mistakes: Not trying common username formats (first.last, flast, etc.)
